steps.assistant_content_head:
I'll create a self-contained HTML slide with the specified neural network concept map, then verify it by rendering to PNG.

First, I'll write the HTML file with inline CSS and SVG:

tool_calls.name: write_file
tool_calls.content_head:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Core Concepts of Neural Networks</title>
    <s

steps.assistant_content_head: I need to render this HTML to a PNG to verify it meets the specifications. Let me use Playwright to render the slide:

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.command: python3 -c "from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright; p=sync_playwright().start(); b=p.chromium.launch(); pg=b.new_page(viewport={'width':1920,'height':1080}); pg.goto('file://' + __import__('o

steps.assistant_content_head: Now I'll examine the rendered PNG to verify if the slide meets all the specifications:

I can see that the slide has been rendered, but there are several issues that need to be addressed:

1. The title "Core Concepts of Neural Networks" is not positioned at y=60px as required
2. The nodes are not arranged in a proper hexagonal layout with the central node at (960px, 540px)
3. The edge labels are overlapping with nodes and each other
4. The text in the nodes appears to be cut off or n
